name: Monitor spend with HTTP / REST API and Twilio end to end

parameters.message: =✅ Monitor spend with HTTP / REST API and Twilio end to end — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Every Few Minutes

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 10 min. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Probe Endpoint A

### Probe Endpoint A

GET https://service-a.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Probe Endpoint B

GET https://service-b.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Probe Endpoint C

GET https://service-c.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Combine Probe Results

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: All Healthy?

### All Healthy?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($json.statusCode || 200) < 400 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Record Uptime Metric · ②→ Already Alerted? (State Check)

### Record Uptime Metric

Appends a new row to 'Asset Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Next: Send Summary to Twilio

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Already Alerted? (State Check)

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Page On-Call via Twilio

### Page On-Call via Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Send Summary to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

# Monitor spend with HTTP / REST API and Twilio end to end

**Pattern:** Threshold Monitoring · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Monitor spend with HTTP / REST API and Twilio end to end. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual portfolio tracking is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Every Few Minutes → Probe Endpoint A → Combine Probe Results → All Healthy? → Record Uptime Metric → Send Summary to Twilio

## Use it for
- Teams that want to threshold monitoring without manual effort
- Anyone automating portfolio tracking in finance
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

'Already Alerted?' uses cross-execution dedupe specifically so a service that's down for an hour pages on-call once, not on every scheduled probe — alert fatigue is a real failure mode, and this node is the guard against it.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a third endpoint to probe and watch the Merge node's input count update.
2. Point a probe at a URL that returns 500 and confirm exactly one page fires, not one per scheduled check.
3. Change the alert dedupe key so alerts re-fire daily instead of once per incident.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your portfolio tracking process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
